mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate environment reduces the cost of capital and encourages risk-taking, leading to higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return for SPX is negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero during periods when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) rises above a critical threshold, it signals persistent inflation, prompting aggressive monetary tightening and eroding corporate purchasing power, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the average return is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of persistently low unemployment (UNRATE) and decelerating job growth (PAYEMS) indicates a late-cycle labor market. This suggests wage pressures may squeeze corporate margins and future hiring will slow, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward return for SPX is positive when UNRATE is below 4.0% and PAYEMS 3-month growth is below 0.1%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic contraction, a positive quarter-over-quarter growth in GDP (GDPC1) signals a potential bottoming or recovery, leading to a short-term positive bounce in equity markets over 5 trading days as sentiment improves.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth turns positive after at least one quarter of negative growth
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or statistically insignificant following positive GDPC1 growth surprises in recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signal aggressive monetary tightening to combat inflation. This leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability, negatively impacting S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following a 75bps increase in FEDFUNDS over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent and robust growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS growth and SPX 20-day forward returns is consistently negative or zero over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous and significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad-based slowdown across manufacturing and construction. This signals weakening economic activity and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:recent declines in housing and industrial activity
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive on average when both industrial production and housing starts show significant declines.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic weakness. This negatively impacts consumer confidence and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods following a 0.5 percentage point increase in UNRATE over 3 months.
